critic_raw:
Review — ceramic defect classifier:

The evaluation uses overall accuracy at 1.4% defect prevalence. This metric is dominated by the majority non-defect class. The apparent improvement (0.5 pp) is within the range achievable by changes in false-positive rate on non-defects, with zero improvement in defect detection. The paper cannot distinguish this scenario from genuine improvement in catching defects.

The paper itself states that escape costs far exceed false-rejection costs. This acknowledgment makes the use of symmetric accuracy even more problematic — the evaluation is explicitly at odds with the stated business priority.

No defect-class metric (recall, precision at threshold, false negatives per million) is reported. Without these, the conclusion that the model reduces escapes is unsubstantiated.

Verdict: critique_wins
adjudication_raw: Baseline run 2. All three must-find issues identified. Verdict: critique_wins.
